source_pr.title: planner: introduce cost trace for cost model ver2
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: ref #36243 Problem Summary: introduce cost trace for cost model ver2 ### What is changed and how it works? Introduce cost trace for cost model ver2. Now the optimizer can output the entire cost formula (or formula of each operator) and costs corresponding to each factor through warnings, which can be used to calibrate and analyze the cost model. And based on it, we can introduce a new column `cost-formula` in `explain` to show how the optimizer calculates costs bottom-up: ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No code Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility Documentation - [ ] Affects user behaviors - [ ] Contains syntax changes - [ ] Contains variable changes - [ ] Contains experimental features - [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```

symptoms: "select * from t where a<4 order by b", (planner/core/plan_cost_ver2_test.go)
failure_signature: "select * from t where a<4 order by b", (planner/core/plan_cost_ver2_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: "select * from t where a<4 order by b", (planner/core/plan_cost_ver2_test.go)
